title: Parts of Body — Monologue
description: Listen, then prove you were listening

_voiceScript.script: Lekár: Takže ste spadli zo schodov. Čo vás bolí? Ivan: Bolí ma hlava, krk a chrbát. Lekár: Bolí vás aj hruď alebo brucho? Ivan: Brucho nie, ale bolí ma hruď a boky. Lekár: Prezriem vám tvár, oči, uši, nos a ústa, po jednom. Dobre? Ivan: Dobre. Lekár: Bolí vás ruka alebo noha? Ivan: Bolí ma rameno a tento malý prst. Bolí ma aj koleno. Lekár: A chodidlá alebo prsty na nohách? Ivan: Nie. Chodidlá sú v poriadku.

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Lekár: Takže ste spadli zo schodov. Čo vás bolí? Ivan: Bolí ma hlava, krk a chrbát. Lekár: Bolí vás aj hruď alebo brucho? Ivan: Brucho nie, ale bolí ma hruď a boky. Lekár: Prezriem vám tvár, oči, uši, nos a ústa, po jednom. Dobre? Ivan: Dobre. Lekár: Bolí vás ruka alebo noha? Ivan: Bolí ma rameno a tento malý prst. Bolí ma aj koleno. Lekár: A chodidlá alebo prsty na nohách? Ivan: Nie. Chodidlá sú v poriadku.

exercises.instruction: Tap every word or expression you heard in the conversation.

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Lekár: Takže ste spadli zo schodov. Čo vás bolí? Ivan: Bolí ma hlava, krk a chrbát. Lekár: Bolí vás aj hruď alebo brucho? Ivan: Brucho nie, ale bolí ma hruď a boky. Lekár: Prezriem vám tvár, oči, uši, nos a ústa, po jednom. Dobre? Ivan: Dobre. Lekár: Bolí vás ruka alebo noha? Ivan: Bolí ma rameno a tento malý prst. Bolí ma aj koleno. Lekár: A chodidlá alebo prsty na nohách? Ivan: Nie. Chodidlá sú v poriadku.

questions.questionEn: The doctor says "Čo vás bolí?" What is he asking?
questions.answerMeaning: "Čo vás bolí?" = What hurts? Bolí comes from the Body Pain lesson and is used to ask about pain.

questions.questionEn: What hurts first after Ivan fell from the stairs?
questions.answerMeaning: "Bolí ma hlava, krk a chrbát" = My head, neck, and back hurt. These body words come from Head Area and Body Pain.

reviewPair.slovak: Bolí ma hlava, krk a chrbát
reviewPair.english: My head, neck, and back hurt

questions.questionEn: Ivan says "Brucho nie, ale bolí ma hruď a boky." What does this mean?

answers.textEn: Not the stomach, but his chest and sides hurt
His stomach hurts, but his chest is fine

questions.answerMeaning: "Brucho nie, ale bolí ma hruď a boky" = Not the stomach, but my chest and sides hurt. Brucho, hruď, and boky are body-area words.

questions.questionEn: Which head-area parts will the doctor inspect?

answers.textEn: Face, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth

questions.answerMeaning: "tvár, oči, uši, nos a ústa" = face, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth. This groups the Head Area vocabulary in one doctor instruction.

reviewPair.slovak: tvár, oči, uši, nos a ústa
reviewPair.english: face, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth

questions.questionEn: When the doctor asks about arm or leg, what does Ivan say hurts?

answers.textEn: His shoulder, little finger, and knee

questions.answerMeaning: "Bolí ma rameno a tento malý prst. Bolí ma aj koleno" = My shoulder and this little finger hurt. My knee hurts too. Rameno, prst, and koleno come from Limbs and Body Pain.

questions.questionEn: What does Ivan say about his feet?
questions.answerMeaning: "Chodidlá sú v poriadku" = The feet are okay. Chodidlá is the anatomical word for feet from the Limbs lesson.

sentences.promptEn: My head, neck, and back hurt.
Not my stomach, but my chest and sides hurt.
Face, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth.
My shoulder, little finger, and knee hurt.
